title: Power Platform And Dataverse APIs - Authentication And Request Conventions

confirmed_facts.fact: The Microsoft Power Platform API is a RESTful web API with request URLs of the form https://api.powerplatform.com/{namespace}/{resource}?api-version={version}.
Power Platform API callers must register an app and obtain authentication tokens for a user or service before calling the API.

Current Power Platform API guidance states the API uses delegated permissions for user-context apps, while service principal identities are granted scoped RBAC roles instead of application permissions.
To use a service principal with Power Platform management APIs, the application must first be registered with Power Platform by an administrator; the service principal cannot register itself.
Dataverse Web API authentication uses OAuth, and Dataverse documentation recommends MSAL for application authentication with the Web API endpoint.

implementation_notes: Distinguish between Power Platform management APIs at api.powerplatform.com or api.bap.microsoft.com and Dataverse environment-specific Web API calls.
For unattended admin automation, model service-principal registration as a prerequisite step rather than assuming app registration alone is sufficient.
Use MSAL-based token acquisition for Dataverse and newer Power Platform integrations.
caveats: Service principal support is not universal across all Power Platform surfaces; some areas still have user-context or product-specific limitations.
Dataverse and Power Platform management APIs share Microsoft Entra authentication patterns but have different endpoint layouts and authorization requirements.
